subject: Reading
read_aloud: Look at this picture. What do you see?
correct_response: Yes! You found a dog, a cat, and a bird!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Look more carefully!
hint: There's also a blue sky and green grass!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The dog is running and chasing the cat!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The dog is sitting still!
hint: Dogs are playful animals that love to chase cats!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The bird is flying and singing!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The bird is on a branch!
hint: Birds fly high in the sky and make nests in trees!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! After the sun sets, it gets dark and we sleep!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The sun rises again in the morning!
hint: Day and night is called a full day because of the sun!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: Where is the sun in the picture?
correct_response: Yes! The sun is in the sky!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Look for the yellow circle!
hint: The sun is the star at the center of our solar system!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! There is grass and flowers on the ground!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The ground might be concrete!
hint: Grass and flowers make the air fresh and beautiful!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What is the weather like in the picture?
correct_response: Yes! It's sunny with a blue sky!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The clouds might be gray!
hint: Sunny days are perfect for playing outside!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: Who is happy in the picture?
correct_response: Yes! The children are smiling and happy!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The children might be sad!
hint: When children are happy, everyone is happy!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What is the cat doing now?
correct_response: Yes! The cat is sleeping peacefully!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The cat is looking at the bird!
hint: Cats sleep a lot! They're nocturnal hunters!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The boy is reading a book!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The boy might be looking at the toys!
hint: Reading books help us learn new things and have adventures!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The flowers are red, yellow, and blue!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Some flowers are purple or white!
hint: Flowers come in many colors just like people have different colored shirts!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The girl is drawing with colorful crayons!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The girl might be painting!
hint: Drawing and coloring are fun creative activities!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What is above the children's heads?
correct_response: Yes! The sun and clouds are above their heads!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The sun is actually very far away!
hint: The sun is a star that's 149.6 million km away!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The boy is holding a toy car!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The boy might be eating the car!
hint: Toy cars come in many colors like red, blue, and green!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The girl is holding a doll!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The doll might be sleeping!
hint: Dolls and toys help children practice taking care of others!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The children are playing in a park!
incorrect_response: Not quite. They might be at school or home!
hint: Parks have playgrounds with swings, slides, and fields!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! They're playing hide and seek!
incorrect_response: Not quite. They might be playing tag!
hint: Hide and seek is great for learning patience and observation!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What time of day is it?
correct_response: Yes! It's morning!
incorrect_response: Not quite. It could be afternoon!
hint: Morning has breakfast, afternoon has lunch, and evening has dinner!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What will the weather be like tomorrow?
correct_response: Yes! It will be sunny, cloudy, and rainy!
incorrect_response: Not quite. It could be stormy!
hint: Weather can change quickly. We should be prepared!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The dad is working on a computer!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The dad might be cooking!
hint: Computers help us do many important things!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The mom is cooking a meal!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The mom might be reading!
hint: Cooking makes delicious food for the family!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The family is eating dinner together!
incorrect_response: Not quite. They might be in different rooms!
hint: Eating together is great family time!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we see at night?
correct_response: Yes! We see stars and the moon at night!
incorrect_response: Not quite. It might be cloudy and we can't see them!
hint: On clear nights, the sky is full of twinkling stars!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we see in the city?
correct_response: Yes! We see buildings, cars, and people!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The city might be empty!
hint: Cities have tall buildings and busy streets with cars and people!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we do on weekends?
correct_response: Yes! We play in parks and have fun on weekends!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might be doing homework!
hint: Weekends are for fun, relaxation, and family time!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! It's winter!
incorrect_response: Not quite. It might be summer!
hint: Winter is cold and snowy. We wear coats and hats!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we do in winter?
correct_response: Yes! We ski, build snowmen, and eat hot chocolate!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might stay inside and watch movies!
hint: Hot chocolate warms us up when we're cold!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What is the season after winter?
correct_response: Yes! It's spring!
incorrect_response: Not quite. It might still be cold!
hint: Spring brings new flowers and baby animals like bunnies and chicks!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we do in summer?
correct_response: Yes! We swim in pools, eat ice cream, and go to the beach!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might get sunburned!
hint: Summer is hot! Don't forget sunscreen and stay hydrated!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we see in autumn?
correct_response: Yes! Leaves turn from green to red and gold!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Some leaves stay green all year!
hint: Farmers harvest crops in autumn before winter comes!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Apples, strawberries, and cherries are red fruits!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Bananas and grapes are yellow!
hint: Red fruits have vitamins and are very healthy!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Bananas, lemons, and pears are yellow fruits!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Oranges and peaches are orange!
hint: Yellow fruits have citrus for vitamin C!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Broccoli, spinach, and peas are green vegetables!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Lettuce and cabbage are also green!
hint: Green vegetables are full of vitamins!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Carrots, pumpkins, and oranges are orange vegetables!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Corn and potatoes are yellow!
hint: Orange vegetables have beta-carotene for vitamin A!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Eggplants and cabbage are purple vegetables!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Onions and garlic are white!
hint: Purple vegetables have antioxidants and add flavor!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! Cauliflower and turnips are white vegetables!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Potatoes are also white!
hint: White vegetables like cauliflower and turnip are used for creamy soups!

subject: Reading
correct_response: Yes! The moon is a circle!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The moon goes through phases!
hint: We see different moon shapes: full moon, half moon, crescent moon!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we use to eat?
correct_response: Yes! We eat with spoons, forks, and knives!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might use our hands!
hint: Utensils help us eat all kinds of food!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we use to drink?
correct_response: Yes! We drink from cups, glasses, and mugs!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might drink from bottles!
hint: Cups and glasses come in many sizes and colors!

subject: Reading
read_aloud: What do we wear on our feet?
correct_response: Yes! We wear shoes, socks, and boots!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We might be barefoot!
hint: Shoes and socks keep our feet warm and protected!
